fixed.title: CRITICAL: DEBUG=True auth bypass removed entirely; startup refuses weak/placeholder secrets outside DEBUG; .env.example added
HIGH: get_booking_details asyncio.gather over 7 queries on one AsyncSession -> sequentialized; concurrency-detecting regression test

MEDIUM: EventPublisher error handling — tenacity retries on transport errors, structured EventPublishError -> 502 'action NOT applied', configurable attempts/timeout (also fixes unused tenacity dep, LOW)
MEDIUM: reassign-client validates booking exists (404) before publishing booking.client_reassigned
MEDIUM: login brute-force lockout (per IP+email, 429), TOTP replay protection (single-use codes), TOTPService fails closed on malformed secrets
MEDIUM: admin_users DDL moved from model docstring to tracked idempotent scripts/admin_users.sql with ownership note + sync test
MEDIUM: zero test coverage -> pytest bootstrapped, 75 tests (ASGI harness with fake Dishka provider, no DB/network needed)

MEDIUM: doc drift — SERVICE_OVERVIEW/API_CONTRACTS/DATA_MODEL/DEPENDENCIES/CLAUDE.md rewritten (publishes events, EVENT_RECEIVER_* env vars, reassign endpoint, receiver failure mode); AUDIT.md rewritten for audit-v2 2026-06-11
LOW: JWT expiry 24h -> 60min default; optional JWT_AUDIENCE/JWT_ISSUER minting + verification (tolerant when unset, mirrors event-users)
LOW: cache invalidation token compared via hmac.compare_digest
LOW: users proxy untyped dict passthrough -> typed allowlist response models; by-ids body -> UsersByIdsRequest (422 not 500); migrated to GET /api/users/by-identity
LOW: change_user_email normalizes new_email lowercase once for validation AND published payload
LOW (security cross-cut): CORS allow_credentials dropped, methods/headers restricted; middleware ordering made explicit with comment + test
LOW: Settings three construction paths -> single get_settings() + create_app() factory
LOW: booking.client_reassigned documented in docs/architecture/MESSAGE_CONTRACTS.md (root repo commit e26157c)

skipped.title: Adopt 'Bearer <key>' scheme for admin ingest API key
skipped.reason: event-receiver's ingest_admin compares the raw Authorization value with hmac.compare_digest (already constant-time after receiver fixer's hardening); switching event-admin unilaterally to Bearer would break the live contract. Kept raw, documented in adapter + .env.example; key strength now enforced at startup on both sides. Needs a coordinated two-side change if desired.

skipped.title: event-receiver/QUEUES_DIGEST.md missing booking.client_reassigned row
skipped.reason: File lives in the event-receiver repo — out of scope for this fixer (do not touch other repos). Root MESSAGE_CONTRACTS.md updated instead; flagged here for the receiver maintainer.

skipped.title: Set DEBUG=False in local .env
skipped.reason: Local gitignored .env keeps DEBUG=True with an explanatory comment because its secrets must match neighbor services' weak local dev values (event-users JWT_SECRET_KEY, event-receiver ADMIN_API_KEY=123) and DEBUG=False would fail the new secret-strength startup check. Harmless now: DEBUG no longer affects authentication. .env.example ships DEBUG=False.

notes: 14 commits on event-admin:audit-fixes (e727b7e..head). Test suite 0 -> 75 (pytest+pytest-asyncio, fake DI provider harness — tests/conftest.py). Breaking-ish changes for ops: (1) startup now fails outside DEBUG if JWT_SECRET_KEY/USERS_SERVICE_API_TOKEN/CACHE_INVALIDATION_TOKEN/EVENT_RECEIVER_API_KEY are <16 chars or placeholders; (2) JWT default lifetime is now 60 min (set JWT_EXPIRE_MINUTES to override); (3) /auth/login can now return 429. Coordination: event-admin now mints aud/iss when JWT_AUDIENCE/JWT_ISSUER are set — enable together with event-users' matching vars (either order is safe, both sides tolerant when unset). event-admin switched user lookup to GET /api/users/by-identity (event-users may drop the deprecated path-segment endpoint once event-notifier also migrates). Publish failures surface as 502 with 'action NOT applied' — no outbox; admin retries manually. LoginGuard/UsersCache are in-memory per-process (single-instance assumption, documented in AUDIT.md).
